title: Will Oscars Trend Of One Film Winning Both Film Editing & Best Picture Continue Three-Year Streak?
text: Throughout Oscar history, 36 instances have occurred where the Film Editing winner also captured Best Picture. The longest consecutive streak lasted seven years, spanning from 1956's Around the World in 80 Days through 1962's Lawrence of Arabia. The Academy has recently demonstrated a pattern of overlap in these categories. The trend of Film Editing pairing with Best Picture has been on a streak once again for the past three years, beginning with Everything Everywhere All at Once (2022), continuing with Oppenheimer (2023), and Anora (2024). This year's Film Editing nominees are F1, Marty Supreme, One Battle After Another, Sinners, and Sentimental Value—all of which also received Best Picture nominations. Sinners achieved exceptional recognition with 16 nominations overall, sweeping the below the line categories with a nomination for every eligible craft. Notably, Hamnet stands apart as the only Best Picture nominee receiving a Director nomination but not a Film Editing nomination, making it a potential trend-breaker.
